Vol. I  ·  No. 26  ·  Tuesday, February 24, 2026  ·  San Francisco, CA

HOME | WORK | POETRY | COMMONPLACE | ABOUT | CONTACT
Profile

Karan Gupta

San Francisco, CA · Born 2006 · He/Him

At a Glance

Current Role
SF Youth Poet Laureate 2024
Research
UCSF Neuroscape Lab
Engineering
Cardinal Robotics — Tech Lead
Founded
PlayKits.org (2022)
Education
San Francisco, CA
Interests
Cognitive neuroscience, robotics, pedagogy, urban poetry

[PROFILE PARAGRAPH PLACEHOLDER — Opening paragraph: introduce Karan as a person who operates across disciplines that most people treat as separate — poetry, engineering, neuroscience, nonprofit work. Set the tone: curious, grounded, specific. Avoid generic "passionate about making a difference" language.]

[PROFILE PARAGRAPH PLACEHOLDER — On growing up in San Francisco: the specific textures of the city, what it means to be a teenager here during a period of rapid transformation. The particular combination of access and inequality. How place shapes a person's questions.]

"I am interested in the questions that fall between disciplines — the ones that poetry can ask but engineering has to answer, or vice versa."

— Karan Gupta

[PROFILE PARAGRAPH PLACEHOLDER — On being a first-generation student navigating elite institutions: what it costs, what it offers, what you learn about yourself in the gap between where you came from and where you're going.]

[PROFILE PARAGRAPH PLACEHOLDER — On PlayKits specifically: why children who have experienced trauma need play as a healing modality, what it looks like to design a kit that meets a child where they are emotionally, and what you've learned about resilience from the families and clinicians you partner with.]

[PROFILE PARAGRAPH PLACEHOLDER — On the Neuroscape research: what drew you to cognitive neuroscience, how it connects to questions about attention and learning that also appear in your poetry, the particular excitement of working at the boundary of what is known.]

[PROFILE PARAGRAPH PLACEHOLDER — On poetry as practice: not as hobby or sideline but as the primary technology for processing experience. What writing teaches you about thinking. The relationship between revision in poetry and debugging in code.]

[PROFILE PARAGRAPH PLACEHOLDER — Closing paragraph: what you're working toward. Not a five-year plan but a direction, a set of questions you want to spend your life getting better at asking.]

Recognition
  • 2024 San Francisco Youth Poet Laureate, appointed by Mayor Daniel Lurie
  • 2024 Featured on KQED and FOX News California as SF Youth Poet Laureate
  • 2024 Performed at Super Bowl press conference
  • 2024 Cardinal Robotics Tech Lead — deployed at Apple, Sutter Health, Cadence Design Systems, and Whole Foods
  • 2023 UCSF Neuroscape Undergraduate Research Fellowship
  • 2022 PlayKits.org — play-based therapy kits for trauma-affected children; 500+ served
